title: General Electric Dryer Timer WE4M532 for Hotpoint HTDX100ED3WW
symptoms_fixed: Dryer runs continuously and will not shut off
consumer_description: This is a genuine OEM Dryer Timer, part number WE4M532, designed to control your dryer's heating and tumbling cycles. The timer dictates the duration of each function, from drying to cool-down. If your dryer's cycles are not advancing or operating correctly, this timer is often the component that needs replacement.
expert_trust_shard: Before you begin the repair, disconnect the dryer from its power source. When removing the old timer, take a clear photo of the wire harness connections with your smartphone before unplugging them. The terminals are lettered and numbered, but a quick photo provides a foolproof reference to prevent mis-wiring the new timer.
html_description_block:
Genuine OEM General Electric Dryer Timer WE4M532

This is a genuine OEM Dryer Timer, part number WE4M532, designed to control your dryer's heating and tumbling cycles. The timer dictates the duration of each function, from drying to cool-down. If your dryer's cycles are not advancing or operating correctly, this timer is often the component that needs replacement.

Fixes These Common Issues:

  • Timer will not advance through cycles
  • Dryer fails to start
  • Dryer shuts off prematurely
  • Dryer runs continuously and will not shut off

Expert Installation Tip:

Before you begin the repair, disconnect the dryer from its power source. When removing the old timer, take a clear photo of the wire harness connections with your smartphone before unplugging them. The terminals are lettered and numbered, but a quick photo provides a foolproof reference to prevent mis-wiring the new timer.

title: General Electric Dryer Fuse Holder WE1M1003
consumer_description: This is a genuine OEM Fuse Holder, part number WE1M1003, for General Electric and Hotpoint dryers. It is designed to securely house the appliance's fuse, protecting the electrical system from power surges and overloads. This component is crucial for the safe operation of your dryer.
expert_trust_shard: Before replacing the fuse holder, always test the fuse itself for continuity with a multimeter. If the old fuse holder is melted or shows signs of burning, this indicates a more significant electrical problem. Simply replacing a melted holder without diagnosing the root cause (like a shorted motor or heating element) will likely result in the new part failing quickly.
html_description_block:
Genuine General Electric Dryer Fuse Holder WE1M1003

This is a genuine OEM Fuse Holder, part number WE1M1003, for General Electric and Hotpoint dryers. It is designed to securely house the appliance's fuse, protecting the electrical system from power surges and overloads. This component is crucial for the safe operation of your dryer.

Fixes Common Dryer Issues:

  • Dryer will not start
  • No power to the dryer
  • Timer does not advance

Expert Installation Tip:

Before replacing the fuse holder, always test the fuse itself for continuity with a multimeter. If the old fuse holder is melted or shows signs of burning, this indicates a more significant electrical problem. Simply replacing a melted holder without diagnosing the root cause (like a shorted motor or heating element) will likely result in the new part failing quickly.

title: GE Hotpoint Dryer Heating Element & Housing Assembly WE11X20397 OEM
symptoms_fixed: No heat or not enough heat
consumer_description: This genuine OEM Heating Element and Housing Assembly, part number WE11X20397, is the core component responsible for generating heat in your General Electric dryer. The assembly includes the heater coil pre-mounted in its metal housing, simplifying installation. If your dryer is tumbling but not heating, this is often the part needed to restore its function.
expert_trust_shard: CRITICAL INSTALLATION TIP: Before you replace this heating element, use a multimeter to test your high-limit thermostat and thermal fuse for continuity. These safety components often fail first when an element burns out due to restricted airflow. Failing to clean your entire vent line from the dryer to the outside of your house will cause the new element to fail prematurely.
html_description_block:
Genuine GE Dryer Heating Element & Housing Assembly WE11X20397

This genuine OEM Heating Element and Housing Assembly, part number WE11X20397, is the core component responsible for generating heat in your General Electric dryer. The assembly includes the heater coil pre-mounted in its metal housing, simplifying installation. If your dryer is tumbling but not heating, this is often the part needed to restore its function.

Fixes Common Dryer Symptoms:

  • No heat or not enough heat
  • Takes too long to dry
  • Dryer is too hot
  • Will not start

Expert Technician Tip:

CRITICAL INSTALLATION TIP: Before you replace this heating element, use a multimeter to test your high-limit thermostat and thermal fuse for continuity. These safety components often fail first when an element burns out due to restricted airflow. Failing to clean your entire vent line from the dryer to the outside of your house will cause the new element to fail prematurely.

Part Number: WE11X20397
Brand: General Electric
Tested On: HTDX100ED3WW

title: GE Hotpoint Dryer Door Latch WE1M1011
consumer_description: This is a genuine OEM Door Latch, part number WE1M1011, for your General Electric or Hotpoint dryer. This component securely fastens the dryer door, engaging the door switch which allows the cycle to begin. It's an essential safety and operational part made of durable plastic and metal components.
expert_trust_shard: Before replacing this latch, check if your dryer door is sagging on its hinges. Try gently lifting the door while you close it. If it latches properly when you lift it, the issue might be worn hinges or door alignment, not a faulty latch. This quick check can save you from buying a part you don't need.
html_description_block:
Genuine OEM Dryer Door Latch WE1M1011

This is a genuine OEM Door Latch, part number WE1M1011, for your General Electric or Hotpoint dryer. This component securely fastens the dryer door, engaging the door switch which allows the cycle to begin. It's an essential safety and operational part made of durable plastic and metal components.

Common Symptoms Addressed:

  • Dryer will not start
  • Door will not stay closed
  • Door will not latch shut

Expert Technician Tip:

Before replacing this latch, check if your dryer door is sagging on its hinges. Try gently lifting the door while you close it. If it latches properly when you lift it, the issue might be worn hinges or door alignment, not a faulty latch. This quick check can save you from buying a part you don't need.
